title: oxytropis retusa var. hidaka-montana
intro: Oxytropis retusa var. hidaka-montana, also known as Hidaka mountain oxytropis, is a type of plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to Japan. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and expectorant, which help to increase urine production and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Oxytropis retusa var. hidaka-montana include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Japanese medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the roots and stems, which are then used to make teas, infusions, and other medicinal products.
